name: plaid
description: Reads Plaid institutions and category metadata through read-only POST endpoints. All credentials and pagination/filter state travel in the JSON request body (Plaid's own convention), driven by a StreamHook.

risk.read: external Plaid API read of institution/category metadata
risk.approval: none; read-only, no reverse-ETL write surface

conformance.reason: StreamHook-driven, in-body pagination and credentials (AUTH_COMPLEX, quarantine.json: 'Plaid's entire API is POST-only with ALL credentials ... and ALL pagination/filter state ... carried in the JSON request body, never in headers or query params'); the engine's declarative read path (engine/read.go's readDeclarative) always calls Requester.Do with a nil body, so a fixture replay keyed on query-string/header matching cannot represent a body-carried request at all -- hook-covered, proven live by internal/connectors/hooks/plaid's own unit tests (real httptest.Server driving the StreamHook's POST body construction and pagination) and internal/connectors/plaid_test.go's pre-existing legacy parity coverage
